articles.title: Developer of education tool Canvas issues apology after hack
articles.content: The data, linked to nearly 9,000 schools worldwide, included student names, email addresses and private messages ⁠between students, teachers, and other staff, the group added. Student newspapers across the United States reported last week the hack caused widespread disruption as students prepared for end-of-year ​tasks and ​assignments.
